channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: More rain forecast for south-east Queensland before temperatures plummet
item.description: Coastal rain will linger today and tomorrow, before clear skies and and chilly nights arrive on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Josh Dye

media:group.media:description: Gloomy weather at Burleigh Heads on Sunday. The forecast improves on Wednesday.

item.title: Sobering-up centre to close less than four years after public intoxication reforms
item.description: A 20-bed facility in the Melbourne suburb of Collingwood will close in 2027 after operating for just a few years following legal reforms.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Eddie

media:group.media:description: After Victoria decriminalised public intoxication in 2023, it established the Collingwood sobering up centre.

item.title: Developer cash flows to Qld politicians as donation ban overturned
item.description: More than $170,000 in donations from developers has been given to Queensland state politicians since laws banning developer donations were overturned in March.
item.dc:creator: Liam Walsh

media:group.media:description: Deputy Premier Jarrod Bleijie oversees state government agency Economic Development Queensland.

item.title: The dilemma Wallabies and All Blacks face when players head overseas
item.description: Both Australia and New Zealand strive to protect Super Rugby Pacific as their premier domestic competition, even it if it has consequences for the Wallabies and All Blacks.
item.dc:creator: Luke Pentony

media:group.media:description: Australia's Len Ikitau (left) is playing for Exeter in the English premiership.

item.title: Queer nightclub in Melbourne 'uninsurable' for two years
item.description: Pride of our Footscray stopped paying public liability insurance when 18 insurers refused to underwrite the venue. The manager says operating without cover keeps staff on tenterhooks.
item.dc:creator: Andi Yu

media:group.media:description: HollyPop hosts drag trivia at a gay institution in Melbourne's west.

item.title: Ex-Labor powerbroker's warning amid NDIS cuts
item.description: A former NSW Labor powerbroker and state disability minister pivotal in advocating for the establishment of the NDIS has warned against rushing through sweeping changes to the scheme.
item.dc:creator: Nas Campanella and Evan Young

media:group.media:description: John Della Bosca warns against rushing through changes to the NDIS.

item.title: Tax is a great motivator but will Labor's changes fix Australia's housing mess?
item.description: The Albanese government and the opposition both want to turn back the clock on the Australian tax system.
item.dc:creator: Alan Kohler

media:group.media:description: Albanese watches on as Jim Chalmers delivers the 2026 federal budget.

item.title: How some countries dodged interest rate rises for years
item.description: As Australians pay more on their mortgages, some countries have had no rise in their interest rate in recent years. So what's going on?
item.dc:creator: Patrick Martin, Doug Dingwall, Claire Campbell, and Raffa Athallah

media:group.media:description: A custom thumbnail graphic image for a story comparing international interest rates. ABC News Graphic: Cordelia Brown

item.title: Push for Australia to extract helium as Iran war impacts global supply
item.description: Helium is needed in health care and to make microchips and semiconductors, but the Middle East war has affected supply. Some think it is an opportunity for Australia's gas industry.
item.dc:creator: Alison Branley

media:group.media:description: Helium is used for more than just party balloons.

item.title: UAE slams 'dangerous escalation' after drone hits near nuclear plant
item.description: Abu Dhabi officials say radiation levels are normal and there was no damage to the plant, but authorities are now working to determine who carried out the attack.

media:group.media:description: Authorities say a drone struck a generator and started a fire near the Barakah nuclear power plant in Abu Dhabi's western desert.

item.title: The science behind why break-ups are so difficult, even when you want to
item.description: It's not just practical and emotional reasons that get in the way of executing a break-up. There are many ways your brain is making you want to stay.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Scott

media:group.media:description: The longer you are in a relationship, the harder it can be to leave.

item.title: Gaza families are still unable to bury dead trapped under rubble
item.description: The families of Palestinians believed to be trapped under the rubble of collapsed buildings want to bury their dead.
item.dc:creator: Bridget Rollason

media:group.media:description: Lina Al-Jaura climbs is still reeling from the loss of her family in an Israeli strike on Gaza.

item.title: Liberal senator speaks out against Taylor's budget reply
item.description: Senator Andrew McLachlan raises concerns over Angus Taylor's use of the term "mass immigration" and plan to push tax-paying migrants off welfare benefits.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Karvelas

media:group.media:description: South Australian senator Andrew McLachlan is among a small group of moderates concerned with the party's direction on migration.

item.title: Denise eats soup to save money, but fears she can't afford private health
item.description: The federal government plans to reduce private health insurance rebates for older Australians, a move one advocate fears could "tip many over the edge".
item.dc:creator: Fiona Blackwood

media:group.media:description: Denise Peters has had private health insurance for 47 years.

item.title: There's a ceasefire in Iran but the war's ripples are still hitting millions
item.description: It has been more than two months since the US and Israel launched strikes in Iran. But the impact of the conflict continues globally.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Morris-Grant and Jessica Riga

item.title: Quiz: It's history time! Where was Pac-Man first released?
item.description: In what year did John Howard become a federal MP? And which country got to play Pac-Man first? Test yourself in this week's history quiz.

media:group.media:description: Pac-Man was first released as an arcade game in 1980.

item.title: The $10b offer Dave Hughes would 'hand on heart' turn down
item.description: Comedian Dave Hughes spoke to The Assembly about why he quit alcohol at 22, being "addicted to work" and the type of dad he's trying to be for his three kids.
item.dc:creator: Fiona Purcell

media:group.media:description: Comedian Dave Hughes says even on his death bed he'll be trying to make people laugh.

item.title: Train driver charged after deadly crash in Thailand
item.description: Thai police have charged a train driver after a crash on Saturday in central Bangkok in which a freight train collided with a public bus killing eight people

media:group.media:description: First responders work at a train crash site near an airport rail link station in Bangkok.

item.title: Ukraine launches massive drone wave against Russia, killing four
item.description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said the attacks were justified as "responses to Russia's prolongation of the war and its attacks on our cities and communities", two days after Russian strikes on Kyiv killed 24.

media:group.media:description: A house burns in the Russian village of Subbotino after it was struck by a Ukrainian drone on Sunday.

item.title: Six shops ordered to shut under new illegal tobacco laws
item.description: More than 140,000 illegal cigarettes have been seized and six stores ordered to close down, days after Western Australia's new tobacco laws came into force.
item.dc:creator: Pip Waller and Andrew Williams

media:group.media:description: This store in Northbridge was one of six issued with a closure notice under WA's new illegal tobacco laws.

item.title: Protesters march at Nakba Day rallies around Australia
item.description: Crowds gather at rallies around the country to commemorate the anniversary of the Nakba, a day that recognises the mass displacement of Palestinians in 1948.

media:group.media:description: Protesters gather in Adelaide's city centre on Sunday to mark Nakba Day.

item.title: Hobart military barracks slated for housing development
item.description: Up to 1,000 new homes could be built on the site of a military barracks in Hobart, under a state-federal redevelopment agreement.
item.dc:creator: Clancy Balen

media:group.media:description: The proposal will replace the Derwent Barracks, which is to be sold as part of a national defence estate sell-off.

item.title: Rousey stops Carano 17 seconds into their double comeback MMA bout
item.description: After an almost 10-year absence from MMA, Ronda Rousey makes light work of Gina Carano in their highly publicised fight in Los Angeles.

media:group.media:description: Ronda Rousey (left) and Gina Carano returned to the cage in los Angeles.

item.title: 'Devoted father and gentleman': Tributes flow for shark attack victim
item.description: The wife of a 38-year-old Perth man who died in a shark attack off the coast of Rottnest Island in Western Australia on Saturday describes him as a devoted father and avid spearfisherman.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Williams and Irena Ceranic

media:group.media:description: Steven Mattaboni, pictured with his wife Shirene and two young daughters, was killed in the shark attack.

item.title: Blues suffer Origin blow, Ponga will 'play anywhere' for Maroons
item.description: NSW has been dealt a State of Origin blow ahead of the Blues' team announcement on Monday, with Latrell Mitchell ruling himself out of Game I selection.
item.dc:creator: Michael Doyle

media:group.media:description: Latrell Mitchell will not play the Origin opener after aggravating his back injury during Magic Round.
